title: Automatic creation of boards
excerpt: Please help me create a board automatically from the saved template

posts.author: Annie_123 (Annie Paul)
posts.cookedHtml: Please help me create a board automatically from the saved template

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Annie_123

there is currently only 1 automation which supports this and is triggered via a status change. Best way to find the automation is to search ‘New Board’

Screen Shot 2021-06-10 at 4.27.19 PM1756×754 102 KB

posts.author: Annie_123 (Annie Paul)
posts.cookedHtml: New board to be created with template

posts.author: Annie_123 (Annie Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
I wanted to have the board creation from the template when an item is created in one board.

Example when project enters project board create a board for that project with the saved template and set start date

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml: Right, when you select create new board you can pick the template from the dropdown. You have to make sure to set the board as a template first and then you can select it from the dropdown

posts.author: Annie_123 (Annie Paul)
posts.cookedHtml: Sure thanks, also wanted to know if I can have two dependency coloumns with respect two different timeline columns in a board.

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Annie_123 I haven’t tried using two dependency columns for different timelines. But in theory that should work… Have you given it a try? I’d be interested in what you find and what your use-case for wanting 2 dependencies for the same item/row is. if you can please share

Best!

posts.author: Annie_123 (Annie Paul)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to have two timelines, one is an actual timeline and the other one is a planned timeline . i want both of them to automatically populate dates based on start date

posts.author: Annie_123 (Annie Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tim,

The same timeline and dependency that worked for one case of mine is not working for another. with one timeline and dependency column can you please help

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Annie_123 just now catching up on this thread.

in regards to the two timeline columns that makes sense, You will just need to have two dependency columns. Also, another interesting feature to check out is the ‘Baseline’ functionality in the gantt chart. It essentially allows you to set the baseline (planned timeline) and you can see that as an overlay on the gantt chart: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360020978159-The-Gantt-chart-baseline

Can you share a screenshot or more details on what the issue is you are facing for the timeline/dependency? 1 thing to check is which ‘automation’ is set up for the dependency, there are different types which could be affecting the behavior :

Screen Shot 2021-06-21 at 11.20.49 AM2336×1566 347 KB

posts.author: Annie_123 (Annie Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tim,

Just wanted to know, if monday charges for using it’s app on different platforms/devices or it is per unique user.

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Annie, its just per user, as far as I know there is no limit on number of devices you can use per user.
